svg.title: Where tensor and pipeline parallelism cross the inter-card link, per decode token, on an 80-layer 70B across two Radeon AI PRO R9700s
svg.desc: A data visualization on a deep navy background. Two horizontal layer rails run left to right from layer 0 to layer 79. The upper rail, labeled tensor parallel, is covered in a dense comb of 160 short amber tick marks, two at every layer, captioned 160 all-reduces per token, about 16 kilobytes each, every tick a cross-card synchronization. The lower rail, labeled pipeline parallel, is empty except for a single tall teal tick at the boundary between layer 39 and layer 40, captioned 1 handoff per token. A right panel titled the link between the cards compares two horizontal bars: PCIe 5.0 x16 at 63 gigabytes per second per direction with high per-collective latency, the link two R9700s share, in amber, and Infinity Fabric on Instinct at 128 gigabytes per second per link across seven fully connected links with low latency, in teal, the link they do not have. A footer credits Megatron-LM, the AMD ROCm MI300 architecture docs, and the PCI Express specification.

text.font-family: ui-sans-serif, system-ui, -apple-system, Segoe UI, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif

svg.text: Decode all-reduce sizing: hidden 8192 x FP16 = ~16 KB per token; 2 x 80 layers = 160 collectives. Prefill scales the payload by the chunk length.
